children.name: Rhinolophus
children.description: Horseshoe bats — named for the elaborate, horseshoe-shaped noseleaf that surrounds their nostrils and acts as a megaphone to focus echolocation calls emitted through the nose rather than the mouth. This allows them to echolocate while chewing prey, and enables extraordinary precision: they can detect a wire 0.08 mm thick in total darkness. Horseshoe bats are constant-frequency echolocators, emitting long calls at a fixed frequency and processing the Doppler shift in the returning echo to detect wing-beat patterns of flying insects. They use Doppler compensation — adjusting the emitted frequency to keep the returning echo at exactly the right pitch. All horseshoe bat species are threatened by declining insect populations and cave disturbance.

children.name: Rhinolophus belligerator
children.description: The Poso horseshoe bat or belligerent horseshoe bat (Rhinolophus belligerator) is an endangered species of horseshoe bat found only on Sulawesi Island, which is part of Indonesia..

children.name: Rhinolophus chaseni
children.description: A small bat known from the north coast of Borneo and adjacent Balabac Island. Named after Frederick Nutter Chasen, curator of the Raffles Museum in Singapore. Inhabits lowland forest near limestone; extremely little is known of its ecology.

children.name: Rhinolophus chiewkweeae
children.description: Chiewkwee's horseshoe bat (Rhinolophus chiewkweeae) is a species of horseshoe bat endemic to the Malay Peninsula..

children.name: Rhinolophus collongensis
children.description: Rhinolophus collongensis - a species in the genus Rhinolophus, family Rhinolophidae.

children.name: Rhinolophus comorensis
children.description: An island endemic found only in the Comoros Archipelago in the Indian Ocean between Mozambique and Madagascar. Inhabits forest and roosts in caves; its isolation has resulted in morphological divergence from mainland African relatives. Considered Vulnerable due to its tiny range.

children.description: Rhinolophus contrarius - a species in the genus Rhinolophus, family Rhinolophidae.

children.description: The Damara horseshoe bat (Rhinolophus damarensis), is a species of bat found in Africa..

children.name: Rhinolophus doriae
children.description: A bat restricted to Borneo, occurring in lowland and hill forest up to 1,500 m elevation. Named after Italian naturalist Giacomo Doria, who made important collections in Borneo. Echolocates at approximately 65–72 kHz.

children.name: Rhinolophus episcopus
children.description: Rhinolophus episcopus - a species in the genus Rhinolophus, family Rhinolophidae.

children.name: Rhinolophus francisi
children.description: Francis's wooly horseshoe bat (Rhinolophus francisi) is a species of bat in the family Rhinolophidae found in Malaysia and Thailand..

children.name: Rhinolophus geoffroyii
children.description: Rhinolophus geoffroyii - a species in the genus Rhinolophus, family Rhinolophidae.

children.name: Rhinolophus gordoni
children.description: Known from Afghanistan, Iran, Oman, and Pakistan, this bat inhabits arid and semi-arid rocky terrain. Roosts in caves and mines in small groups. It is morphologically similar to R. clivosus and was long included within that species.

children.name: Rhinolophus gorongosae
children.description: Rhinolophus gorongosae - a species in the genus Rhinolophus, family Rhinolophidae.

children.name: Rhinolophus gorontalensis
children.description: Described from the Gorontalo region of northern Sulawesi, Indonesia. A poorly known Wallacean endemic with very few records. Its noseleaf morphology and echolocation parameters distinguish it from other Sulawesi rhinolophids.

children.name: Rhinolophus grivensis
children.description: Rhinolophus grivensis - a species in the genus Rhinolophus, family Rhinolophidae.

children.name: Rhinolophus hanaki
children.description: Rhinolophus hanaki - a species in the genus Rhinolophus, family Rhinolophidae.

children.name: Rhinolophus hilli
children.description: Rhinolophus hilli, Hill's horseshoe bat, is a species of bat in the family Rhinolophidae..

children.name: Rhinolophus hillorum
children.description: The Hills' horseshoe bat (Rhinolophus hillorum) is a species of bat in the family Rhinolophidae..

children.name: Rhinolophus hipposiderus
children.description: Rhinolophus hipposiderus - a species in the genus Rhinolophus, family Rhinolophidae.

children.name: Rhinolophus horaceki
children.description: The Horáček's horseshoe bat (Rhinolophus horaceki) is a species of bat in the family Rhinolophidae, which is endemic to Cyrenaica, Libya..

children.name: Rhinolophus indorouxii
children.description: Rhinolophus indorouxii is a species of horse-shoe bat in the family Rhinolophidae that is found in Southern India..

children.name: Rhinolophus inops
children.description: #REDIRECT Philippine forest horseshoe bat.

children.name: Rhinolophus kahuzi
children.description: The Kahuzi horseshoe bat (Rhinolophus kahuzi) is a species of bat in the family Rhinolophidae, found in eastern Democratic Republic of the Congo..

children.name: Rhinolophus lakkhanae
children.description: Rhinolophus lakkhanae - a species in the genus Rhinolophus, family Rhinolophidae.

children.name: Rhinolophus lanosus
children.description: The northern woolly horseshoe bat (Rhinolophus perniger) is a bat species of the family Rhinolophidae..

children.name: Rhinolophus lemanensis
children.description: Rhinolophus lemanensis - a species in the genus Rhinolophus, family Rhinolophidae.

children.name: Rhinolophus lobatus
children.description: A large East African horseshoe bat sometimes treated as a subspecies of R. hildebrandtii. Found in highland forests and savanna woodland of Ethiopia, Kenya, and Tanzania. Its noseleaf sella has a distinctive peaked profile used in species identification.

children.name: Rhinolophus luetus
children.description: Rhinolophus luetus - a species in the genus Rhinolophus, family Rhinolophidae.

children.name: Rhinolophus madurensis
children.description: The Madura horseshoe bat (Rhinolophus madurensis) is a species of bat from the family Rhinolophidae..

children.name: Rhinolophus maghrebensis
children.description: Rhinolophus maghrebensis - a species in the genus Rhinolophus, family Rhinolophidae.

children.name: Rhinolophus mcintyrei
children.description: McIntyre's horseshoe bat (Rhinolophus mcintyrei) is a species of horseshoe bat that is endemic to Papua New Guinea..

children.name: Rhinolophus microglobosus
children.description: Rhinolophus microglobosus is a species of horseshoe bat found in Southeast Asia..

children.name: Rhinolophus minor
children.description: Rhinolophus minor - a species in the genus Rhinolophus, family Rhinolophidae.

children.name: Rhinolophus mixtus
children.description: Known from a small number of specimens from Sulawesi, Indonesia. The species name reflects its intermediate morphological features between other Wallacean Rhinolophus. Almost nothing is known of its ecology, echolocation, or population status.

children.name: Rhinolophus montanus
children.description: The Timorese horseshoe bat (Rhinolophus montanus) is a species of bat endemic to Timor-Leste..

children.name: Rhinolophus monticolus
children.description: Rhinolophus chutamasae or the mountain horseshoe bat is a species of bat in the family Rhinolophidae..

children.name: Rhinolophus morio
children.description: The Malaysian woolly horseshoe bat or Jester's horseshoe bat (Rhinolophus morio) is a bat species of the family Rhinolophidae..

children.name: Rhinolophus mucus
children.description: A poorly known bat from the Khasi Hills of northeastern India and adjacent Myanmar. Described in the early 20th century and rarely recorded since. Its taxonomic status relative to other northeast Indian rhinolophids is uncertain pending molecular analysis.

children.name: Rhinolophus murinus
children.description: Rhinolophus murinus - a species in the genus Rhinolophus, family Rhinolophidae.

children.name: Rhinolophus namuli
children.description: Rhinolophus namuli - a species in the genus Rhinolophus, family Rhinolophidae.

children.name: Rhinolophus nanus
children.description: Rhinolophus nanus - a species in the genus Rhinolophus, family Rhinolophidae.

children.name: Rhinolophus perniger
children.description: The northern woolly horseshoe bat (Rhinolophus perniger) is a bat species of the family Rhinolophidae..

children.name: Rhinolophus petersii
children.description: A poorly known East African species sometimes treated as synonymous with R. clivosus. Described by German naturalist Wilhelm Peters from material collected on the East African coast. Roosts in caves and rock crevices in coastal forest areas.

children.name: Rhinolophus priscus
children.description: Rhinolophus priscus - a species in the genus Rhinolophus, family Rhinolophidae.

children.name: Rhinolophus proconsulis
children.description: The Bornean woolly horseshoe bat or Proconsul's horseshoe bat (Rhinolophus proconsulis) is an endangered species of horseshoe bat found on Borneo..

children.name: Rhinolophus pumilio
children.description: Rhinolophus pumilio - a species in the genus Rhinolophus, family Rhinolophidae.

children.name: Rhinolophus pumilus
children.description: A small bat of the Philippines, Sulawesi, and the Moluccas, sometimes lumped with R. pusillus but recognised as distinct by most recent authorities. Inhabits forest and limestone karst; echolocates at approximately 113–120 kHz.

children.name: Rhinolophus refulgens
children.description: Rhinolophus refulgens, or the glossy horseshoe bat, is a species of horseshoe bat in the genus Rhinolophus and the family Rhinolophidae described by Knud Andersen in 1905.

children.name: Rhinolophus rhodesiae
children.description: The Bushveld horseshoe bat (Rhinolophus simulator) is a species of bat in the family Rhinolophidae..

children.name: Rhinolophus rufogenys
children.description: A Philippine endemic with distinctive reddish facial coloration around the cheeks and noseleaf. Found on Luzon and several smaller Philippine islands. Very little biological information is available; the species has been rarely collected.

children.name: Rhinolophus schnitzleri
children.description: Schnitzler's horseshoe bat (Rhinolophus schnitzleri) is a species of bat in the family Rhinolophidae..

children.name: Rhinolophus sedulus
children.description: The lesser woolly horseshoe bat (Rhinolophus sedulus) is a species of bat in the family Rhinolophidae.

children.name: Rhinolophus silvestris
children.description: The forest horseshoe bat (Rhinolophus silvestris) is a species of bat in the family Rhinolophidae..

children.name: Rhinolophus spec
children.description: Rhinolophus spec - a species in the genus Rhinolophus, family Rhinolophidae.

children.name: Rhinolophus suillus
children.description: A small bat of the Philippines found primarily in Mindanao and the Sulu Archipelago. Named for its rather pig-like noseleaf appearance at certain angles. Inhabits lowland rainforest; ecology and echolocation remain poorly known.

children.name: Rhinolophus tatar
children.description: #REDIRECT Sulawesi broad-eared horseshoe bat.

children.name: Rhinolophus thailandensis
children.description: The Thailand horseshoe bat (Rhinolophus thailandensis) is a horseshoe bat endemic to northern Thailand..

children.name: Rhinolophus tiomanensis
children.description: Described from Pulau Tioman off the east coast of peninsular Malaysia, though also found on the Malay Peninsula and in Borneo. Separated from R. stheno on morphological and echolocation grounds. Echolocates at approximately 89–93 kHz.

children.name: Rhinolophus truncatus
children.description: Rhinolophus truncatus - a species in the genus Rhinolophus, family Rhinolophidae.

children.name: Rhinolophus tylopus
children.description: A bat of peninsular Malaysia and Borneo, found in lowland and peat swamp forest. The species name means 'knob-footed' in reference to a distinctive tarsal feature. Calls at approximately 60–65 kHz and is associated with undisturbed lowland forest.

children.name: Rhinolophus vietus
children.description: A tiny bat described from Vietnam and also recorded in adjacent Laos and China. Among the smallest members of the genus; echolocates at approximately 115–120 kHz. Inhabits limestone karst caves and surrounding tropical forest.

children.name: Rhinolophus vulgaris
children.description: Rhinolophus vulgaris - a species in the genus Rhinolophus, family Rhinolophidae.

children.name: Rhinolophus wenzensis
children.description: Rhinolophus wenzensis - a species in the genus Rhinolophus, family Rhinolophidae.

children.name: Rhinolophus willardi
children.description: Willard's horseshoe bat (Rhinolophus willardi) is a newly described species of bat in the family Rhinolophidae.

children.name: Rhinolophus xinanzhongguoensis
children.description: Rhinolophus xinanzhongguoensis, the wedge-sellaed horseshoe bat or southwestern China horseshoe bat, is a species of horseshoe bat from China.

children.name: Rhinolophus yatesi
children.description: A newly described species from northern Borneo, separated from related taxa on the basis of echolocation frequency and morphometrics. Inhabits foothill and submontane forest around limestone karst. Its discovery highlights the continuing undescribed bat diversity on Borneo.

children.name: Rhinolophus yongyuthi
children.description: Rhinolophus yongyuthi - a species in the genus Rhinolophus, family Rhinolophidae.

children.name: Rhinolophus yunanensis
children.description: Dobson's horseshoe bat (Rhinolophus yunanensis) is a species of bat in the family Rhinolophidae..

children.name: Rhinolophus yunnanensis
children.description: Described from Yunnan Province in southwestern China; also recorded in Myanmar and Thailand. Inhabits subtropical forest and roosts in limestone caves. Echolocates at approximately 70–75 kHz and is closely related to R. pearsonii.

children.name: Vaylatsia
children.description: Vaylatsia - a genus in the family Rhinolophidae.

children.name: Vaylatsia prisca
children.description: Vaylatsia prisca - a species in the genus Vaylatsia, family Rhinolophidae.
